items.title: Three marketing personas from behavior features
items.scenario: A streaming app has session count, watch hours, and cancellation attempts per user—but no predefined persona labels. Product picks k=3, scales features, runs k-means, and names clusters 'binge watchers', 'weekend viewers', and 'trial churn risks' after reviewing centroids.
items.explanation: Centroids summarize average behavior per segment. k=3 matches an actionable campaign strategy; cluster IDs are validated against business intuition before targeting.

items.title: Image color quantization
items.scenario: A designer compresses a photo palette by treating each pixel's RGB values as a 3D point and clustering with k=16. Each cluster centroid becomes one palette color; every pixel is replaced by its nearest centroid color.
items.explanation: k-means finds representative colors (centroids) that minimize total squared color distance—classic vector quantization with a fixed budget of k colors.

items.title: Document grouping for support triage
items.scenario: Support tickets are embedded into 384-dimensional vectors. The team clusters embeddings with k=8 to surface eight recurring issue themes, then routes new tickets to the nearest centroid's queue.
items.explanation: High-dimensional embeddings work when distances are meaningful (often after L2 normalization). Centroids act as theme prototypes; k=8 reflects eight known product areas.
